title: 〜たほうがいい・〜ないほうがいい
description: 〜ほうがいい gives advice or states a preference — "it's better to ~." Use the past た form to recommend doing something, and the plain ない form to recommend not doing it.

content.text: ほう means "side" or "option," so 〜ほうがいい literally says "the ~ option is good." To advise someone to do something, attach it to the past た form of the verb. This is one of the most common ways to give practical suggestions.

content.japanese: verb (past た form) ＋ ほうがいい
肉（にく）は焼（や）いたほうがいい。
content.english: It's better to grill the meat.

content.japanese: アイスクリームを買（か）ったほうがいいです。
content.english: It's better to buy ice cream.

content.japanese: 疲（つか）れているなら、早（はや）く寝（ね）たほうがいいですよ。
content.english: If you're tired, you'd better go to bed early.

content.japanese: 日本（にほん）から帰（かえ）ったほうがいいです。
content.english: It's better to head back from Japan.

content.text: To advise against doing something, attach ほうがいい to the plain ない form. This recommends NOT doing the action.

content.japanese: verb (plain ない form) ＋ ほうがいい
content.english: it's better not to ~ / you shouldn't ~

content.japanese: 無理（むり）しないほうがいい。
content.english: It's better not to overdo it.

content.japanese: 夜遅（よるおそ）くコーヒーを飲（の）まないほうがいいですよ。
content.english: You shouldn't drink coffee late at night.

content.text: With nouns, 〜のほうが (without いい fixed on the end) simply marks the preferred side in a comparison, often paired with より ("than").

content.japanese: 自分（じぶん）で作（つく）ったうどんのほうがおいしい。
content.english: The udon I made myself is tastier.

content.text: Watch the た/ない asymmetry: the affirmative advice uses the PAST た form (早（はや）く寝（ね）たほうがいい), but the negative advice uses the plain PRESENT ない form (飲（の）まないほうがいい) — NOT the past 〜なかったほうがいい. So it is 食（た）べたほうがいい / 食（た）べないほうがいい, never 食（た）べなかったほうがいい. Also don't use the present 食（た）べるほうがいい for advice; that reads as a general preference, not a recommendation.

肉（にく）は焼（や）いたほうがいい。
examples.english: It's better to grill the meat.

examples.japanese: 日本（にほん）から帰（かえ）ったほうがいいです。
examples.english: It's better to head back from Japan.

examples.japanese: verb (plain ない form) ＋ ほうがいい
examples.english: it's better not to ~ / you shouldn't ~
